title: MessageQueryInput: add text and threadRootId filters
description:
Why: MessageQueryInput is missing two JMAP Chat spec-defined filters — text (full-text search over body) and threadRootId (thread-scoped results). Without them callers cannot request text search or thread-scoped views.

What: Add two optional fields to MessageQueryInput<'a>: text: Option<&'a str> and thread_root_id: Option<&'a str>. Update message_query() to conditionally insert "text" and "threadRootId" into the filter JSON object when Some (pattern: if let Some(t) = input.text { filter.insert("text".into(), t.into()); }). Add a test asserting that text and threadRootId appear in the body_json matcher.

Algorithm:
1. Open crates/jmap-chat/src/methods/mod.rs
2. Locate MessageQueryInput<'a> struct (search for 'pub struct MessageQueryInput')
3. Add two new fields after has_attachment:
   - pub text: Option<&'a str>,
   - pub thread_root_id: Option<&'a str>,
4. Open crates/jmap-chat/src/methods/message.rs
5. Locate the filter-building block (search for 'filter.insert')
6. Add after the has_attachment block:
   if let Some(t) = input.text { filter.insert("text".into(), t.into()); }
   if let Some(tid) = input.thread_root_id { filter.insert("threadRootId".into(), tid.into()); }
7. Run cargo test -p jmap-chat to verify no regressions
8. Run cargo clippy and cargo fmt

Defensive rules: text and threadRootId are optional (not required); do not add them to the filter object if None (must not send null — servers may reject unknown null fields per RFC 8620). No validation of the string content (server enforces unsupportedFilter if not supported). No unwrap(). No credentials.

Tests: Add one new test (or extend test 11) that sends message_query with text="hello" and verifies body_json matcher shows 'text': 'hello' in the filter. This is a wiremock body matcher test.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p jmap-chat passes (all 29+ tests); cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes; cargo fmt --check passes; new test verifying text filter in body_json
